---
title: After-Hours Visitor ID Verification
type: post_order
community: Sierra Ridge
priority: high
effective_date: 2026-05-14
source: manual_entry
status: draft
tags:
  - visitor-access
  - id-verification
  - after-hours
last_updated: 2026-05-14
version: 1.0
---

## Summary

Overnight guards at Sierra Ridge must verify a physical photo ID for all after-hours visitors before granting access.

## Details

If a visitor only presents a photo of an ID, the guard must call the resident before allowing entry and document the exception in the visitor log.

## Required Actions

- Verify physical photo ID for all after-hours visitors.
- Call the resident before entry when only a photo of an ID is available.
- Record any exception in the visitor log.

## QA and Risk Notes

This post order affects access control and should be reviewed before activation.

## Open Questions

- Confirm the exact after-hours time window for Sierra Ridge.

## Change History

- 1.0 initial draft from manual entry on 2026-05-14.

## Summary

The Harbor Point east gate arm stayed open after a vendor truck exited on 2026-05-14 around 07:45.

## Incident Details

Patrol secured the gate manually and notified maintenance.

## Follow-Up Required

- Confirm the gate controller has been repaired.
- Update this incident after maintenance confirms resolution.

## QA and Risk Notes

This incident affects access control and requires follow-up verification.

## Open Questions

- Who from maintenance received the notification?
- What time was the gate controller repaired?

## Change History

- 1.0 initial draft from shift note on 2026-05-14.

---
title: Operations Form Ingestion Automation
type: automation
community: global
priority: medium
effective_date: 2026-05-14
source: planning_note
status: draft
tags:
  - n8n
  - ingestion
  - github
last_updated: 2026-05-14
version: 1.0
---

## Summary

A future n8n workflow should accept operations form webhook submissions, classify each note, write a Markdown file to the vault, and commit the change to GitHub.

## Details

This is a Phase 2 workflow design note only. It does not implement ChromaDB, RAG, Open WebUI, or advanced automations.

## Required Actions

- Define the webhook payload.
- Connect the prompt chain for classification, routing, normalization, and QA risk checking.
- Write the final Markdown to the routed vault folder.
- Commit the resulting file to GitHub on master.

## QA and Risk Notes

Validate filenames, required metadata, and folder routing before enabling unattended writes.

## Open Questions

- Confirm which operations form will send the webhook.

## Change History

- 1.0 initial draft from planning note on 2026-05-14.
